transcript:
Hi everyone, I'm Josip. I'm a developer and founder on Solana since 2021, and over the last couple of years, I've helped over 200 founders raise over 6 million dollars in capital through VCs, hackathons, and grants and so forth. And as I was helping these teams raise millions, I figured that there is one thing that I love doing the most. Essentially, I became good at fundraising as I love people asking money. And I want to build a platform where other founders can also raise capital.

Now we have a platform like that in Web2, it's called Kickstarter. In the last 15 years, Kickstarter launched successfully 300,000 projects with over 9 billion dollars pledged. Now, this number is actually correct, but when you think about it in respect to Web3, 15 years of Kickstarter effort equal to 9 Fartcoin launches.

Furthermore, 90% of users quit from using Kickstarter after backing just two projects. This is because there is a lot of downsides when backing projects early. Creator fails to deliver, creator rugs, and so forth, so forth. So Kickstarter turned more into like a presale mechanism where creators have to put in all the effort, create a project, and then spend 20,000 dollars on advertising to raise successfully.

So, I'm building Fundl. Fundl is also a crowdfunding platform, but we match the downsides of backing projects early by matching with the tangible upside. So if you back a project on Fundl, you may also profit if they sell later successfully. So it's kind of like Kickstarter, but in a silly way, it's a Kickstarter with Ponzinomics. If you back the project early, you profit.

The way it works is you go to Fundl... let's assume you're a video game developer. You go to Fundl to raise the first 50,000 dollars to develop a game prototype. And you raise from the first 200 believers. As you raise there and develop the prototype, you go to Steam or Kickstarter and raise another half a million in a form of a presale. If you raise successfully, people that backed you early on Fundl and helped you fund the research and development, profit... share the profits.

We've just launched the first project which raised 8,000 dollars in under one hour, which is an animated comic by an Emmy winner. And now we're raising for a second project which is a crypto-themed poker set. So you essentially have like an orange chip which is themed with Bitcoin, blue one is USDC, black is Solana. And essentially you can visit fundl.fun and grab yourself a crypto-themed poker set and profit if it later sells on even more. And that's kind of it. Thank you.

# Base–Solana Bridge Goes Live with Chainlink CCIP, Unlocking Massive Cross-Chain Liquidity
![Liam Turner](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns=%22http://www.w3.org/2000/svg%22%20viewBox=%220%200%2048%2048%22%3E%3C/svg%3E)
By[Liam Turner](https://www.cryptoninjas.net/authors/liam/)
December 6, 2025
* 3min read
![base-solana](https://www.cryptoninjas.net/wp-content/uploads/base-solana.jpeg)
**Key Takeaways:**
* The Base–Solana Bridge is now live on mainnet, secured jointly by Chainlink CCIP and Coinbase.
* Users and developers can transfer SOL and SPL tokens to Base apps, enabling unified liquidity across both ecosystems.
* The launch marks one of the most significant cross-chain expansions for Base, strengthening its position as a hub for multichain Web3 activity.
The long-anticipated Base–Solana Bridge has officially[launched](https://x.com/chainlink/status/1996657107597160887?s=20), creating one of the most consequentia

# CoinBase and Solana Launch Cross-Chain Bridge Using Chainlink Protocol
Base and Solana launch Chainlink-secured bridge enabling cross-chain asset transfers. SOL and SPL tokens now tradeable on Base apps with $13.5B combined value.
![Trader Edge](https://coincentral.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/cropped-whFHHq4W-32x32.jpg)By[Trader Edge](https://coincentral.com/author/trader/)December 5, 2025**3 Mins Read
![](https://coincentral.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/coinbase.jpg "CoinBase and Solana Launch Cross-Chain Bridge Using Chainlink Protocol")
## TLDR
* Base and Solana launched a bridge using Chainlink’s Cross-Chain Interoperability Protocol to enable asset transfers between the two networks
* The bridge allows users to trade Solana tokens and SPL assets on Base apps like Zora, Aerodrome, and Virtuals
* Solana holds $9 billion in locked value as the second-largest blockchain while Base ranks sixth with $4.5 billion
* This marks a technical milestone connecting Ethereum Virtual Machine chains with Solana’s different architecture
* SOL and LINK tokens both dropped 3% following the announcement
💥Find the Next KnockoutStock!
Get live prices, charts, and KO Scores from[KnockoutStocks.com](https://knockoutstocks.com), the data-driven platform ranking every stock by quality and breakout potential.
Coinbase’s Base network and Solana have connected through a new bridge that allows users to move assets between the two blockchains. The bridge went live on mainnet this week using Chainlink’s Cross-Chain Interoperability Protocol.
> > Base meets Solana. Connected by Chainlink.
> > The Base-Solana Bridge is now live & secured by CCIP, unlocking native Solana asset support on Base and unified liquidity between both ecosystems.
[> @Base
](https://twitter.com/base?ref_src=twsrc^tfw)> 🤝Chainlink 🤝[> @Solana
](https://twitter.com/solana?ref_src=twsrc^tfw)[> https://t.co/iaXe4GYDJx
](https://t.co/iaXe4GYDJx)> [> https://t.co/Zaf3SoPtpY
](https://t.co/Zaf3SoPtpY)> [> pic.twitter.com/2SbI0P2oeO
](https://t.co/2SbI0P2oeO)
> > — Chainlink (@chainlink) [> December 4, 2025
](https://twitter.com/chainlink/status/1996657107597160887?ref_src=twsrc^tfw)
The new infrastructure enables users to trade Solana-based tokens on Base applications without leaving the Base environment. Apps including Zora, Aerodrome, Virtuals, Flaunch, and Relay have integrated the bridge for their users.
Base developers can now add support for[Solana](https://coincentral.com/solana-etfs-face-largest-ever-outflow-as-sol-price-tests-key-support-levels/)assets like SOL and SPL tokens directly in their applications. The bridge code is open-source and available on GitHub for a

tags.impact_reason: Fundl is an interesting idea, but Demo Day pitches generally have low immediate impact on the Solana ecosystem.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a futuristic fountain or 'wishing well' as the Semantic Anchor because it perfectly represents the concept of 'crowdfunding' – many individuals contributing to a central pool. The innovation of Fundl is the 'profit-sharing,' which I've visualized as a powerful geyser of light erupting back out of the well. For the homepage text, I integrated a holographic '%' symbol directly into this geyser of light. This is a direct, high-impact reference to the 'revenue share' and 'profit' that differentiates Fundl from traditional platforms like Kickstarter, making the core value proposition instantly clear in a diegetic way.
visual_idea.description: A macro shot focusing on the surface of the liquid energy within the digital fountain. We see several small, glowing streams of light plunging into the iridescent surface, creating intricate, glowing ripples. The reflection of the powerful, unseen upward geyser casts a brilliant, dominant light across the frame, hinting at the immense energy and potential return on the investment. The focus is on the moment of contribution and its immediate, energetic effect.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A tight macro shot, perfectly centered and symmetrical, filling the 1:1 square frame with intricate texture. The focus is intently on the iridescent surface of the bioluminescent liquid energy within a digital fountain. We witness several small, glowing holographic data streams, vibrant with a cool, directional bioluminescent glow, plunging into this iridescent surface. This interaction creates mesmerizing, intricate, glowing ripples that spread outwards. A powerful, unseen upward geyser, reflecting brilliantly, casts a dominant, uniform white light across the entire frame from above, hinting at immense energy and potential return on investment. The composition emphasizes the precise moment of contribution and its immediate, energetic effect. The surrounding elements of the digital fountain subtly incorporate iridescent metal textures, reflecting the vibrant light and glows. 8k, octane render, raytracing, physically based rendering (PBR), hyper-detailed, intricate textures, extreme depth of field, futuristic lighting.

editorial.headline: Fundl Brings Profit-Sharing to Crowdfunding on Solana Mainnet
editorial.standfirst: By tokenizing early-stage product backing, Fundl addresses the retention crisis in Web2 crowdfunding. The platform allows users to fund R&D in exchange for revenue share from future downstream raises.
key_takeaways: Fundl introduces a 'profit-sharing' model where early backers earn upside if the project later succeeds on platforms like Kickstarter or Steam.
Founder Josip Volarević, a veteran Solana developer, previously helped ecosystem teams raise over $6M in capital.
The platform's pilot—an animated comic by an Emmy winner—filled its $8,000 allocation in under one hour.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The legacy crowdfunding model is broken. According to data presented at Solana Breakpoint, 90% of **Kickstarter** users churn after backing just two projects. The friction is obvious: backers assume 100% of the execution risk—often facing delays or 'rugs'—while their upside is capped at receiving the product itself. **Fundl**, a new platform launched by developer **Josip Volarević**, aims to invert this dynamic by introducing financial upside to the earliest stage of capital formation.

Fundl operates on a two-stage financing mechanism designed to align incentives between creators and backers. In the initial phase, creators raise a small tranche of capital (e.g., $50,000) on Fundl to fund immediate R&D, such as a game prototype or physical mold. If the project proceeds to a successful larger presale on Web2 platforms like **Steam** or **Kickstarter**, the early Fundl backers receive a share of the profits. Volarević describes this as "matching the downsides of backing projects early with tangible upside," effectively tokenizing the 'believer' capital that currently acts as a donation in Web2.

For institutional investors, the 'Alpha' here is the application of crypto-economic incentives ('Ponzinomics,' as Volarević tongue-in-cheekily notes) to real-world commerce. The platform utilizes Solana’s high-throughput rails to automate the distribution of yields to micro-investors—a process that would be operationally prohibitive in traditional finance. The model has already demonstrated traction: the platform's first pilot, an animated comic by an Emmy-winning creator, raised $8,000 in under 60 minutes. The current live demonstration, a crypto-themed poker set, serves as a proof-of-concept for how physical goods can be bootstrapped on-chain before hitting mass-market distribution channels.
